subject: reading

contentProfile.primarySkill: Understanding erosion processes and Earth's geological features
contentProfile.assignmentFormat: Study Guide with sections on key concepts
sourceEvidence: matched science term: erosion
Erosion happens when wind, water, or ice wear away rocks and soil
Rivers and rain can carry tiny pieces of rock from mountains to beaches or oceans
The color of sand comes from the rocks nearby
Water and wind break down the rocks and move them to the beach
Maps show land and water like mountains, rivers, lakes, and oceans
A compass rose shows directions: North, South, East, West
Rivers usually flow downhill from mountains to oceans or lakes
Rivers start high in the mountains and flow down to lower places like lakes or oceans
Water always flows downhill, never up
If you float on a river, you'll end up in a lake or ocean
Rounded hills are smaller and softer than pointy peaks
Pointy peaks are tall and sharp — like big mountains
Use clues and directions (North, South, East, West) to find locations on a map
